volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.6.1258 - Section 4.7.1310)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.6.1258
sections.original: Nescio ubi hic prope adest quem expeto videre : olet profecto .
sections.translation: He is somewhere or other close at hand whom I do so long to behold . I ’ m sure I smell him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1259
sections.original: Naso pol iam haec quidem plús videt quam óculis .
sections.translation: Troth , now , she really sees better with her nose than with her eyes .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1259b
sections.translation: She is blind from love .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1260
sections.translation: Prithee , do support me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1260b
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1260c
sections.translation: Lest I should fall .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1260d
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1260e
sections.translation: Because I cannot stand ; my senses — my senses are sinking so by reason of my eyes .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1261b
sections.translation: ( aloud .) Heavens ! you ’ ve seen the Captain .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1262b
sections.translation: I have .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1262c
sections.translation: I don ’ t see him . Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1262d
sections.translation: Troth , you would see him if you were in tove .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1263
sections.original: Non edepol tu illum magis amas , quam ego amem , si per te liceat .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you don ’ t love him more than I do myself , with your good leave .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1264
sections.original: Omnes profecto mulieres te amant , ut quaeque aspexit .
sections.translation: No doubt all of the women , as soon as each has seen you , are in love with you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1265
sections.original: Nescio , tu ex me hoc audiveris an non : nepos sum Veneris .
sections.translation: ( to PALAESTRIO .) I don ’ t know whether you have heard it from me or not ; I ’ m the grandson of Venus .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1266
sections.original: Mea Milphidippa , adi obsecro et congredere .
sections.translation: My dear Milphidippa , prithee do approach and accost him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1266b
sections.translation: How she does stand in awe of me !

sections.label: Section 4.6.1267
sections.translation: She is coming towards us .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1267b
sections.translation: I wish to speak with you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1267c
sections.translation: And we with you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1267d
sections.translation: I have brought my mistress out of the house , as you requested me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1268b
sections.translation: So I see .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1268c
sections.translation: Request her , then , to approach .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1269
sections.original: Induxi in animum , ne oderim item ut alias , quando orasti .
sections.translation: Since you have entreated it , I have prevailed upon my mind not to detest her just like other women .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1270
sections.original: Verbum edepol facere non potis , si accesserit prope ad te .
sections.translation: I ’ faith she wouldn ’ t be able to utter a word if she were to come near you ; while she was looking at you , her eyes have in the meantime tied her tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1272
sections.translation: I see that this woman ’ s disorder must be cured .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1272b
sections.translation: See how terrified she is since she beheld you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1273b
sections.original: Víri quoque armáti idem istuc faciunt ,
sections.translation: Even armed men are the same ; don ’ t wonder at a woman being so . But what does she wish me to do ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1275
sections.original: Ad se ut eas : tecum vivere volt atque aetatem exigere .
sections.translation: You to come to her house ; she wishes to live and to pass her life with you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1276
sections.original: Egon ád illam eam , quae nupta sit ? vir eius me deprehendat .
sections.translation: What !— I come home to her , when she is a married woman ? Her husband is to be stood in fear of .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1277
sections.original: Quin tua causa exegit virum ab sé .
sections.translation: Why ,— for your sake , she has turned her husband out of her house .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1277b
sections.translation: How ? How could she do so ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1278
sections.translation: The house was her marriage-portion .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1278b
sections.translation: Was it so ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1278c
sections.translation: It was so , on my word .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1278d
sections.translation: Bid her go home ; I ’ ll be there just now .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1279b
sections.translation: Take care , and don ’ t keep her in expectation ; don ’ t torment her feelings .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1280b
sections.translation: Not I , indeed . Do you go then .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1280c
sections.translation: We are going . ( ACROTELEUTIUM and MILPHIDIPPA go into the house of PERIPLECOMENUS .)

sections.label: Section 4.6.1281
sections.translation: But what do I see ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1281b
sections.translation: What do you see ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1281c
sections.translation: See there , some one is coming , I know not who , but in a sailor ’ s dress .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1282b
sections.original: It ad nos , volt te profecto .
sections.translation: He is surely wanting us , now ; really , it is the shipmaster .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1283b
sections.translation: He ’ s come , I suppose , to fetch her .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1283c
sections.translation: I fancy so .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1284
sections.original: Alium alio pacto propter amorem ni sciam
sections.translation: Did I not know that another man in other ways

sections.label: Section 4.7.1285
sections.translation: has done many a thing unbecomingly on account of love , I should be more ashamed by reason of love for me to be going in this garb . But since I have learned that many persons by reason of love have committed many actions , disgraceful and estranged from what is good , ⋯ for I pass by how Achilles suffered [*] his comrades to be slain —

sections.label: Section 4.7.1290
sections.original: sed eccúm Palaestrionem , stat cum milite :
sections.translation: But there ’ s Palaestrio , he ’ s standing with the Captain . My talk must now be changed for another kind . Woman is surely born of tardiness itself . For every other delay , which is a delay just as much , seems a less delay than that which is on account of a woman .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1295
sections.translation: I really think that this is done merely from habit . But I shall call for this Philocomasium . I ’ ll knock at the door then . Hallo ! is there any one here ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1297b
sections.translation: Young man — what is it ? What do you want ? Why are you knocking ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1298b
sections.translation: I ’ m come to inquire for Philocomasium ; I ’ m come from her mother . If she ’ s for going , let her set off .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1300
sections.translation: She is delaying us all ; we wish to weigh anchor .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1301
sections.original: Iam dudum res paratast . i , Palaestrio ,
sections.translation: Her things have been some time in readiness . Hearkye , Palaestrio , take some assistants with you to carry to the ship her golden trinkets , her furniture , apparel , all her precious things . All the articles are already packed up which I gave her .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1305
sections.translation: I ’ ll go .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1305b
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , do make haste .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1305c
sections.translation: There shall be no delay . Pray , what is it that has been done [*] with your eye ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1307
sections.translation: Troth , hut I have my eye .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1307b
sections.translation: But the left one I mean .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1307c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . On account of the sea , I use this eye less ; but if I kept away from the sea [*] , I should use the one like the other .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1310
sections.translation: But they are detaining me too long .
